prompt: Write a complete Adams CMD script for a cam-follower mechanism. Create a cam body (1 kg, Ixx=Iyy=Izz=100 kg·mm²) fixed to ground at the origin and a follower (0.5 kg rigid body, Ixx=Iyy=50 kg·mm², Izz=10 kg·mm²) that can only translate along the global X axis via a translational joint. Apply a single-component force using STEP5 that pushes the follower in the +X direction: the force should ramp smoothly from 0 N to 500 N as the follower's DX displacement (from .model.follower.tip_mkr relative to .model.cam.ref_mkr) goes from 0 mm to 20 mm, then hold at 500 N beyond 20 mm. Add a velocity-dependent damping of 2.0 × VX. Add simple geometry shapes (cylinder or box) to both the cam and follower parts. Simulate for 1 second.

assertions.text: Script creates a model with at least two parts: a cam (fixed to ground) and a follower
A translational joint constrains the follower to translate along one axis
STEP5 is used for the cam force (not plain STEP)
STEP5 x-range spans 0 to 20 (follower displacement from 0 to 20 mm)
STEP5 output range spans 0 to 500 (force from 0 to 500 N)
Damping term + 2.0 * VX(...) added to the force expression
Full FUNCTION= expression wrapped in double quotes
At least one geometry shape is added to each part (cam and follower)
All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
Script uses simulation single_run transient command
CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
